title: Functional Fabric Fair Spring 2026 concludes with strong turnout
summary:
Functional Fabric Fair Spring 2026 in Portland drew strong global participation, showcasing next-generation sustainable textiles, biobased materials and advanced fabric innovations. 
With 300+ suppliers and major brands attending, the event highlighted practical, scalable solutions, industry collaboration and growing momentum towards circular, high-performance apparel.

title: India’s March WPI inflation 4.91% for textile manufacturing, RMG 1.81%
summary:
India's wholesale price inflation (WPI) rose to a 38-month high of 3.88 per cent in March, provisional data show. 
Manufactured products inflation rose to 3.39 per cent from 2.92 per cent in February. 
The WPI inflation for manufacturing of textiles rose to 4.91 per cent in March from February’s 3.29 per cent. 
The same for wearing apparel dropped to 1.81 per cent in March from February’s 2.14 per cent.

title: Bangladesh forms cabinet committee to adjust power prices amid crisis
summary:
Bangladesh recently formed a six-member cabinet committee to recommend the necessary power tariff adjustment at the wholesale and retail levels amid the Middle East crisis, global oil price volatility and surging power generation costs. 
The committee will examine the necessity of revising electricity tariffs in light of global instability affecting the domestic energy sector.

title: Global shocks drive rise in energy policy activity, govt spending: IEA
summary:
Several recent global shocks have triggered an exceptionally active period of energy policymaking, sharpening governments’ focus on energy security, resilience and affordability—and driving public spending on energy significantly higher, the International Energy Agency said. 
The energy crisis now could trigger a new dynamic phase of policymaking, similar to the responses seen during the 1970s oil crises.

title: Canada suspends fuel excise tax on gasoline, diesel, aviation fuels
summary:
Canadian PM Mark Carney yesterday announced temporarily suspending the federal fuel excise tax on gasoline, diesel and aviation fuels across the country. 
Starting April 20, the government will suspend the full amount of the tax on gasoline and diesel until September 7, 2026. 
The move is expected to reduce Canadians’ bills at the gas station by 10 cents per litre on regular gasoline and 4 cents on diesel.

title: AMANN to present 6 new sewing & embroidery threads at Texprocess 2026
summary:
AMANN will showcase six new sewing and embroidery threads at Texprocess 2026, led by award-winning AeoniQ Fil, a biodegradable, cellulose-based thread supporting circular design. 
Innovations include Smart Stitch, reflective threads, recycled UV-resistant yarns and water-soluble options, reinforcing sustainability, performance and advanced textile solutions.

title: China's central bank injects 500 bn yuan via reverse repo
summary:
China’s central bank injected ¥500 billion (~$72.89 billion) via a six-month outright reverse repo to maintain liquidity.
With ¥600 billion (~$87.47 billion) maturing this month, the move signals a net withdrawal.
The operation highlights the PBOC’s calibrated approach, using its expanded policy toolkit to balance liquidity amid evolving financial conditions.

title: ASEAN+3 sees macroeconomic stabilisation amid structural adjustments
summary:
Macroeconomic stabilisation in the ASEAN+3 nations has progressed while navigating the new energy shock triggered by the Middle East conflict. 
Structural adjustments continue, according to the latest ASEAN+3 Regional Economic Outlook. 
While vulnerabilities remain, the pace and direction of policy adjustment have improved significantly in the region.

title: Italian firms to showcase innovations at INDEX 2026 Geneva
summary:
Italian textile machinery firms will highlight advanced nonwoven technologies at INDEX 2026 in Geneva from May 19-22. 
Organised by ICE and ACIMIT, the Italy Pavilion will feature sustainable, customised solutions. 
Despite global challenges, Italy remains a key exporter, with the sector generating €2.1 billion (~$2.48 billion) and exporting around 86 per cent of output.

title: Kornit brings global apparel leaders together at Konnections 2026
summary:
Kornit’s Konnections 2026 gathers 500+ global leaders to advance on-demand apparel production. 
With Atlas MATRIX and PrintFactory, the company is building a digital infrastructure linking demand, production, and fulfilment. 
The event underscores a shift towards agile, real-time manufacturing with reduced inventory risk and scalable global consistency.

title: UK’s Sosandar returns to profitability amid robust FY26 performance
summary:
Sosandar reported FY26 revenue of £42.3 million (~$57.53 million), up 14 per cent, driven by strong online growth, with own-site sales rising 24 per cent. 
The company returned to profitability with PBT of £0.4 million (~$0.54 million) and improved margins. 
Despite slightly missing revenue expectations, performance remained solid. 
Strong third-party sales supported confidence in profitable growth.

title: Germany’s Puma appoints James Carnes to new creative leadership role
summary:
Puma has appointed James Carnes as senior vice president creative direction.
Reporting to Maria Valdes, he will oversee creative direction, innovation, and product excellence. 
With over two decades of experience, including leadership roles at Adidas, he will align design strategy with business goals to strengthen Puma’s global brand appeal and market position.

title: Ireland’s Primark boosts circular fashion with updated strategy
summary:
Primark has launched Circular Product Standard 2.0 to expand circular fashion across its range. 
The framework focuses on durability, sustainable materials and recyclability, covering nine product categories. 
In FY 24/25, 5 per cent of products were circular by design, including 20 per cent of jersey. 
A new ‘Progressive’ level raises standards, backed by training and practical design improvements.

title: Hong Kong apparel imports stabilise after sharp dip last year
summary:
Hong Kong’s apparel imports showed early stabilisation in January–February 2026, rising 0.7 per cent YoY to $946.49 million after a steep 2025 decline. 
Casualwear led gains, with T-shirts and jerseys increasing share, while structured wear softened. 
Despite this uptick, imports remain well below 2024 levels, reflecting cautious sourcing and weak global demand.

title: FTAs, PTAs in focus as Sri Lanka aims for growth
summary:
Industry stakeholders highlight that Sri Lanka faces a competitive disadvantage in global markets due to limited preferential trade agreements, unlike many competitors, making securing free trade agreements (FTAs) and preferential trade agreements (PTAs) critical. 
Industry stakeholders call upon the government to accelerate negotiations on FTAs and PTAs.

title: Italy’s apparel export-import plunge after positive trend in 2025
summary:
Italy’s sharp decline in apparel trade in early 2026 reflects short-term demand caution and inventory tightening rather than structural weakness.
Strong 2025 performance highlights resilient trade fundamentals supported by diversified markets.
Gradual sourcing shifts, including reduced reliance on China, indicate evolving supply chain strategies.

title: US' Kontoor Brands appoints Erinn Murphy to lead finance role
summary:
Kontoor Brands has named Erinn Murphy VP, global head of finance & operations for Helly Hansen and Corporate Investor Relations, starting May in Oslo. 
She joins from Crocs, Inc., bringing nearly two decades of experience across investor relations, strategy and equity research. 
Michael Karapetian will expand his role and return in Q3 2026 to support transition and investor engagement.

title: Switzerland’s apparel imports grow double-digit in Jan–Feb 2026
summary:
Switzerland’s apparel imports rose 11.7 per cent year on year (YoY) to $1.523 billion in January–February 2026, signalling steady demand. 
Growth builds on 2025 momentum, led by knitwear and comfort-led segments. 
China, Bangladesh and Italy remain key suppliers. 
Strong purchasing power and stable retail trends continue to support consistent sourcing activity.

title: Marks & Spencer renews strategic partnership with TCS
summary:
Marks and Spencer have renewed its long-standing strategic partnership with TCS to accelerate its technology transformation.
The collaboration will focus on building an omnichannel, data-driven retail model powered by AI and modern technologies, enhancing customer experience while supporting long-term growth and operational agility. 
It will also strengthen digital capabilities across core business function

title: Trade bodies call for moving HR 4930 forward in US legislative process
summary:
AAFA along with 18 other trade bodies recently wrote to Congressional leadership in the US House of Representatives seeking support to move HR 4930 forward in the legislative process. 
The piece of legislation, aimed at addressing long-standing challenges to the enforcement of IPR at US borders, was reported with unanimous, bipartisan support from the House Ways and Means Committee.
